titleStmt.title: The History of Mark the Less
The Paradise

sourceDesc.p: Ernest A. Wallis Budge, The Book of Paradise, Being the Histories and Sayings of the Monks and Ascetics of the Egyptian Desert by Palladius, Hieronymus and Others: The Syriac Texts, According to the Recension of ʿAnân-Îshôʿ of Bêth ʿÂbhê, Edited with an English Translation, vol. 1, Lady Meux Manuscript 6 (London: W. Drugulin, 1904), p. 278.

Transcription of the text downloaded from Wikisource on 10/27/2024: https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/The_Paradise/Volume_1/Book_2/Chapter_43 . This Wikisource transcription is available under a Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike License

body.head: Chapter 43: Of Abba Mark the Less, the Disciple of Abba Sylvanus
body.ab: They used to say concerning Abbâ. Sylvanus that he wished to depart to Syria, and his disciple Mark said unto him, Not only do I not wish thee to depart from this place, O father, but I will not permit thee to go [now]; wait then here for three days more; and on the third day Mark died in peace.
